### Python: Create New PTY

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a new Pseudo-Terminal (PTY) with specified configurations including size, user, working directory, environment variables, and timeouts. It returns a `CommandHandle` object for interacting with the new PTY. Dependencies include `typing` and specific types like `PtySize` and `Username`.

```python
from typing import Optional, Dict

# Assuming PtySize and Username are defined elsewhere
# from some_module import PtySize, Username, CommandHandle

def create(size: PtySize,
           user: Username = "user",
           cwd: Optional[str] = None,
           envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
           timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
           request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle:
    """Start a new PTY (pseudo-terminal)."""
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Pty Module Methods (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.4/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Contains methods for managing pseudo-terminals (PTYs) in the sandbox. `kill` terminates a PTY by its PID. `send_stdin` sends byte data to a PTY's input. `create` starts a new PTY with specified size, callbacks, user, and environment. `resize` adjusts the dimensions of an existing PTY. These methods may involve timeouts and depend on types like `PtySize`, `OutputHandler`, `PtyOutput`, `Username`, `Dict`, and `AsyncCommandHandle`.

```python
async def kill(self, pid: int, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> bool:
    """Kill PTY."""
    pass
```

```python
async def send_stdin(self, pid: int,
                     data: bytes,
                     request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
    """Send input to a PTY."""
    pass
```

```python
async def create(
        self,
        size: PtySize,
        on_data: OutputHandler[PtyOutput],
        user: Username = "user",
        cwd: Optional[str] = None,
        envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
        request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> AsyncCommandHandle:
    """Start a new PTY (pseudo-terminal)."""
    pass
```

```python
async def resize(self, pid: int,
                 size: PtySize,
                 request_timeout: Optional[float] = None):
    """Resize PTY."""
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### sendInput()

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.2/commands/page.mdx

Sends input data to a PTY. This is used to simulate user input to a running process.

```APIDOC
## POST /sendInput

### Description
Sends input data to a PTY. This is used to simulate user input to a running process.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sendInput

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **pid** (number) - Required - process ID of the PTY.
- **data** (Uint8Array) - Required - input data to send to the PTY.
- **opts** (object) - Optional - connection options, specifically `requestTimeoutMs`.
  - **requestTimeoutMs** (number) - Optional - connection options.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "pid": 12345,
  "data": "aGVsbG8gd29ybGQ=" // Base64 encoded "hello world"
  "opts": {
    "requestTimeoutMs": 5000
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
None (returns void)

#### Response Example
(No response body for success)
```

--------------------------------

### User for Running Command in E2B SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.0/commands/page.mdx

This option specifies the username under which the command should be executed. If not provided, the command will run as the default user. This is part of the `CommandStartOpts` interface.

```typescript
optional user: Username;
// Default: user
```

--------------------------------

### Set Command User

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.1/commands/page.mdx

Specifies the user under which a command should be executed. This option is part of the CommandStartOpts interface and defaults to the user who started the command.

```typescript
optional user: Username;

// Default
// user
```

### PTY Module: Create PTY (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) session with specified size, user, working directory, environment variables, and timeouts. Returns a CommandHandle for interaction.

```python
def create(size: PtySize,
           user: Username = "user",
           cwd: Optional[str] = None,
           envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
           timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
           request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle:
    """Start a new PTY (pseudo-terminal)."""
    pass
```

### Create a New PTY

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.5/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Initiates a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) session within the sandbox. This function allows customization of the PTY's size, user, working directory, and environment variables. It returns a `CommandHandle` for further interaction with the newly created PTY. Connection and request timeouts can be specified.

```python
def create(size: PtySize,
           user: Username = "user",
           cwd: Optional[str] = None,
           envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
           timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
           request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle
```

--------------------------------

### Set Command User - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/commands/page.mdx

Specifies the username under which the command should be executed. Defaults to the user who started the command.

```typescript
optional user: Username;
```

--------------------------------

### CommandStartOpts Interface - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/commands/page.mdx

The `CommandStartOpts` interface defines the configuration options for starting a new command. It includes settings for background execution, working directory, environment variables, callbacks for stdout/stderr, request timeouts, command timeouts, and the user to run the command as. Some properties have default values.

```typescript
interface CommandStartOpts {
  background?: boolean;
  cwd?: string;
  envs?: Record<string, string>;
  onStderr?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  onStdout?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  requestTimeoutMs?: number;
  timeoutMs?: number;
  user?: Username;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Interact with Pseudo-Terminals (PTY) (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Provides functionalities to manage pseudo-terminals (PTYs) within the sandbox environment. This includes creating, killing, sending input to, and resizing PTY sessions. It requires `PtySize`, `Username`, `Optional`, `Dict`, `CommandHandle` types.

```python
class Pty()

```

```python
def kill(pid: int, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> bool
```

```python
def send_stdin(pid: int,
               data: bytes,
               request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None
```

```python
def create(size: PtySize,
           user: Username = "user",
           cwd: Optional[str] = None,
           envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
           timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
           request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle
```

```python
def resize(pid: int,
           size: PtySize,
           request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None
```

### Python Pseudo-Terminal (PTY) Operations

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.2/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Enables interaction with pseudo-terminals within the sandbox. Supports killing PTYs, sending input, creating new PTYs with specified configurations, and resizing PTYs. Requires PtySize and Username types.

```python
class Pty():
    def kill(pid: int, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> bool:
        # Kill PTY.
        pass

    def send_stdin(pid: int,
                   data: bytes,
                   request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
        # Send input to a PTY.
        pass

    def create(size: PtySize,
               user: Username = "user",
               cwd: Optional[str] = None,
               envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
               timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
               request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle:
        # Start a new PTY (pseudo-terminal).
        pass

    def resize(pid: int,
               size: PtySize,
               request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
        # Resize PTY.
        pass
```

### WatchOpts Interface Properties (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.3.0/filesystem/page.mdx

Defines options for watching a directory. This interface extends `FilesystemRequestOpts` and adds specific parameters for watch operations. It includes an optional `onExit` callback, a `recursive` flag to watch subdirectories, a `timeoutMs` for the watch operation, and inherited options for request timeouts and user context.

```ts
onExit?(): void | Promise<void>;
recursive?: boolean;
requestTimeoutMs?: number;
timeoutMs?: number;
user?: Username;
```

--------------------------------

### Set Command Working Directory

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.1/commands/page.mdx

Specifies the working directory for a command to be executed. If not provided, the command runs in the user's home directory. This option is part of the CommandStartOpts interface.

```typescript
optional cwd: string;

// Default
// home directory of the user used to start the command
```

--------------------------------

### Create PTY - Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.6/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Initializes a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) within the sandbox. It requires specifying the PTY's size and allows configuration of user, working directory, and environment variables. It returns a CommandHandle for interacting with the new PTY.

```python
from typing import Dict, Optional
from e2b.api.subprocess import CommandHandle, Username, PtySize

def create(size: PtySize,
           user: Username = "user",
           cwd: Optional[str] = None,
           envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
           timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
           request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle
```

--------------------------------

### Pty API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.2/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Enables interaction with pseudo-terminals (PTYs) within the sandbox, including creation, manipulation, and sending input.

```APIDOC
## Pty API

This module allows for managing and interacting with pseudo-terminals (PTYs) in the sandbox.

### `kill`

Terminates a specific PTY process.

#### Method

`kill(pid: int, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None)`

#### Parameters

- `pid` (int) - Required - The Process ID of the PTY to kill.
- `request_timeout` (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

#### Returns

`bool` - `true` if the PTY was killed, `false` if the PTY was not found.

### `send_stdin`

Sends data as input to a specified PTY.

#### Method

`send_stdin(pid: int, data: bytes, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None)`

#### Parameters

- `pid` (int) - Required - The Process ID of the PTY.
- `data` (bytes) - Required - The input data to send.
- `request_timeout` (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

#### Returns

`None`

### `create`

Starts a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) within the sandbox.

#### Method

`create(size: PtySize, user: Username = "user", cwd: Optional[str] = None, envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, timeout: Optional[float] = 60, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None)`

#### Parameters

- `size` (PtySize) - Required - The desired size (rows and columns) of the PTY.
- `user` (Username) - Optional - The user to run the PTY as (defaults to "user").
- `cwd` (str) - Optional - The working directory for the PTY.
- `envs` (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Environment variables to set for the PTY.
- `timeout` (float) - Optional - Timeout for the PTY session in seconds (defaults to 60).
- `request_timeout` (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

#### Returns

`CommandHandle` - A handle to interact with the newly created PTY.

### `resize`

Resizes an existing PTY to a new size.

#### Method

`resize(pid: int, size: PtySize, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None)`

#### Parameters

- `pid` (int) - Required - The Process ID of the PTY to resize.
- `size` (PtySize) - Required - The new size (rows and columns) of the PTY.
- `request_timeout` (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

#### Returns

`None`
```

--------------------------------

### Set Working Directory for Command in E2B SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.0/commands/page.mdx

This option specifies the working directory for a command. If not provided, the command will run in the home directory of the user who started it. This is part of the `CommandStartOpts` interface.

```typescript
optional cwd: string;
// Default: // home directory of the user used to start the command
```
